title: Huberantha trichoneura
taxonomy: Huberantha trichoneura is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The classification of this species has been studied and researched by botanists, who have placed it within the genus Huberantha. Further classification includes its order, Magnoliales, and its class, Magnoliopsida. This classification system helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

side_effects: Huberantha trichoneura is a plant species that may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals due to the presence of certain compounds. The sap of the plant can cause skin redness, itching, or rashes, which can be uncomfortable but typically resolve on their own. In rare cases, exposure to the plant's sap can lead to more severe reactions, such as blistering or swelling. It's essential to handle the plant with care and avoid touching the sap to minimize potential side effects. If you experience any unusual symptoms after exposure to Huberantha trichoneura, seek medical attention for proper evaluation and treatment.
intro: Huberantha trichoneura, also known as the "African Black Elder," is a medicinal plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Annonaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and act as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha trichoneura include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to possess cytotoxic and anti-proliferative activities. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, although it can also be found in capsule or tincture form.
